title: ferrotorch-core: signal::taylor diverges from scipy.signal.windows.taylor by 1.5e-3 (LSL-level) — surfaced by phase 2.7 (#769)
description:
ferray-window 0.3.6 taylor(M=16, nbar=4, sll=30, norm=true) returns values that differ from scipy.signal.windows.taylor by ~1.5e-3 at every position.

Sample: index 0 → ferrotorch 0.25388183826768423 vs scipy 0.252321041674507 (delta 1.561e-3).

This is likely an algorithmic-implementation difference in the Taylor weighting calculation rather than rounding — a 1.5e-3 delta is too coarse to be FP error.

Possible upstream issues: ferray-window may use a different convention for the SLL conversion (R = 10^(sll/20) vs 10^(-sll/20)) or a different normalization formula.

Mitigation in phase 2.7 conformance: cascade_skip for window_taylor; we still cover every other window function (parzen, tukey, hanning, etc.) at F64_WINDOW = 1e-6 which all pass.
